purpose: Add the lib/context seams the S2 invitation card and S3 preview will consume: a side-effect-free pre-join Welcome group-data decode, a MarmotContext read boundary for it, a shared pubkey-truncation util, and a pure inviter-attribution helper. No UI, no store-shape changes, no relay publish.

public_api.name: readPreJoinGroupData
public_api.signature: readPreJoinGroupData(welcomeRumor: UnwrappedRumor, marmotClient: MarmotClient): Promise<{ name: string; description: string; adminPubkeys: string[] } | null>
public_api.description: Sibling of readPreJoinGroupName in app/src/lib/marmot/welcomeSubscription.ts. Same side-effect-free key-package loop against the Welcome via readWelcomeMarmotGroupData (never readInviteGroupInfo — its extensions are empty in this release). Returns the wider MarmotGroupData slice {name, description, adminPubkeys}. Returns null on any failure or when no local key package matches (AC-DATA-1, AC-DATA-2). No key-package burn, no MLS adoption, no network publish.

public_api.name: readPreJoinGroupName
public_api.signature: readPreJoinGroupName(welcomeRumor: UnwrappedRumor, marmotClient: MarmotClient): Promise<string | null>
public_api.description: PRE-EXISTING export, refactored to delegate to readPreJoinGroupData ((await readPreJoinGroupData(...))?.name ?? null) so the key-package loop is not duplicated. Signature and behavior for existing callers (auto-accept correlation) unchanged.

public_api.name: MarmotContext.getInvitationGroupData
public_api.signature: getInvitationGroupData(welcomeEventJson: string): Promise<{ name: string; description: string; adminPubkeys: string[] } | null>
public_api.description: NEW MarmotContext method (added to the interface, provider value, useMemo deps, and DEFAULT_MARMOT no-op default). JSON.parses the stored rumor string into UnwrappedRumor (returns null on parse failure, never throws), then dynamic-imports welcomeSubscription.ts and calls readPreJoinGroupData with clientRef.current, mirroring the acceptPendingInvitation dynamic-import boundary. Exposed via useMarmot(). Returns null immediately if clientRef.current is unset.

public_api.name: truncatePubkey
public_api.description: Pure function extracted from the inlined truncation at PendingInvitations.tsx:80-81 into new module app/src/lib/pubkeyDisplay.ts. Returns hex.slice(0,8)+'…'+hex.slice(-8) when hex.length >= 16, otherwise hex unchanged.

public_api.name: resolveInviterLabel
public_api.signature: resolveInviterLabel(inviterPubkeyHex: string, ownPubkeyHex: string | null | undefined): string
public_api.description: Pure helper in app/src/lib/pubkeyDisplay.ts. Returns getContact(inviterPubkeyHex, ownPubkeyHex)?.nickname when that lookup yields a non-empty string; otherwise returns truncatePubkey(inviterPubkeyHex). Both 'no known contact' (null) and 'known contact with empty nickname' ('') fall through to the truncated pubkey. getContact is local-only (reads stored contacts, no relay call) — see AC-ATTR-3.

dependencies_allowed: @internet-privacy/marmot-ts (readWelcomeMarmotGroupData, getWelcome, getWelcomeKeyPackageRefs — already imported by welcomeSubscription.ts)
app/src/lib/pendingInvitations.ts (PendingInvitation type only, already imported by welcomeSubscription.ts; store itself unchanged)
dependencies_forbidden: No relay-publish or broadcast call of any kind
No new import into app/src/components/groups/InvitationCard.tsx (does not exist yet — out of scope)
No import of welcomeSubscription.ts directly into any React component (components only reach it through MarmotContext.getInvitationGroupData)
test_targets: app/tests/unit/lib/pubkeyDisplay.test.ts (truncatePubkey: known-length, short/edge-length; resolveInviterLabel: known contact w/ nickname, known contact w/ empty nickname, unknown pubkey, seeded via the real contacts.ts/contactCache.ts local-storage-mock pattern used by tests/unit/contacts.test.ts — no mocked getContact)
